title: deserialize_chunk_data allocates base64 string before size check
description: deserialize_chunk_data calls String::deserialize (allocating the full base64 string), then B64.decode (allocating the decoded Vec<u8>), and only then checks len > 10 MiB. A hostile peer sending a 14 MiB base64 string causes allocation of ~14 MiB (string) + ~10 MiB (decoded Vec) = ~24 MiB before rejection. The guard is at the decoded side but the string is allocated first. Fix: check the base64 string's byte length before decoding (base64 len * 3/4 >= limit implies rejection).
